Title: A functionally conserved STORR gene fusion in Papaver species that diverged 16.8 million years ago

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: STORR fusion protein (cytochrome P450–oxidoreductase reticuline epimerase)
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Fusion protein comprising a cytochrome P450 module (CYP82Y2-like) and an oxidoreductase/COR-like module linked by a conserved 9–13 aa linker
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Gateway step to promorphinan/morphinan biosynthesis; possibly also aporphine (R)-glaucine-related branch in P. californicum
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes epimerization of (S)-reticuline to (R)-reticuline through its fused P450 and oxidoreductase modules
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for promorphinan and morphinan biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as the gateway/first committed step enabling promorphinan/morphinan biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Multiple Papaver species; yeast strain G175 for heterologous assays
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Gal10 promoter in pESC-TRP for yeast heterologous expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in a subset of Clade 2 Papaver species; detected in young leaf transcriptomes including P. californicum
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected by transcriptomics in Clade 2 Papaver species containing STORR; assayed as microsomal protein in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Promorphinans/morphinans (e.g., salutaridine, thebaine, codeine, morphine); possibly (R)-glaucine contextually
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Morphinans include morphine, a powerful analgesic; glaucine has reported bronchodilator, anti-inflammatory and neuroleptic activities, and (R)-glaucine has reported serotonin-efficacy effects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of (R)-reticuline and thereby enables promorphinan/morphinan biosynthesis; P. californicum STORR is functionally conserved despite absence of promorphinans/morphinans
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plants grown under controlled long-day glasshouse conditions; metabolite analysis in juvenile plant latex and pre-dehiscence capsules
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metabolite profiling across species; transcriptomics; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae; microsomal enzyme assays with (S)-reticuline; chiral LC-MS epimer analysis; phylogenetic and synteny analyses
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and genome assembly data under BioProject PRJNA770669; raw reads SRR16389878–SRR16389886, SRR16690173, SRR16690174, SRR16591802–SRR16591806; assembled transcriptomes GJOO00000000–GJOY00000000; genomes JAJJMA000000000, JAJJMB000000000, JAJJMC000000000, JAJJWW000000000, JAJJWX000000000
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STORR is a fused CYP82Y2-like/oxidoreductase gene essential for the epimerization of (S)- to (R)-reticuline, the first committed step toward promorphinan/morphinan biosynthesis. 2. Functional STORR orthologs were identified in multiple Clade 2 Papaver species, including P. californicum, and P. californicum STORR converted (S)-reticuline to (R)-reticuline in yeast microsomes. 3. Phylogenetic analysis supports a single origin of the STORR fusion between 16.8 and 24.1 MYA before divergence of P. californicum from other Clade 2 Papaver species. 4. Despite retaining functional STORR, P. californicum does not accumulate promorphinans/morphinans, implying STORR may also contribute to other BIA branches such as (R)-glaucine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A foundational engineering target for reconstructing or transferring morphinan pathway capacity; should be stacked with downstream promorphinan genes (SALSYN, SALR, SALAT, THS) because STORR alone is insufficient for morphinan accumulation; useful as an evolutionary and synthetic biology gateway enzyme for BIA branch switching
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No activity was detected with (S)-glaucine or (S)-laudanosine substrates in vitro. Functional conservation does not guarantee morphinan accumulation because downstream genes may be absent or lost.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream of STORR; early committed branch after (R)-reticuline
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms a promorphinan intermediate toward morphinans
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Orthologues identified only in a subset of Clade 2 Papaver species producing promorphinan/morphinan compounds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in transcriptomic/genomic analyses in species with promorphinan pathway capacity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports promorphinan formation in species containing the promorphinan gene set
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled long-day growth; metabolites measured in latex and capsules
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative metabolite profiling; transcriptomics; genome synteny and orthology analyses
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and genome data under BioProject PRJNA770669
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SALSYN encodes salutaridine synthase and catalyzes the conversion of (R)-reticuline to salutaridine. 2. Orthologues were detected only in a subset of Clade 2 Papaver species that contain STORR and produce promorphinan/morphinan compounds. 3. Genomic synteny indicates SALSYN became clustered with STORR and other promorphinan genes before divergence of P. bracteatum and P. somniferum.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required downstream module to pair with STORR for engineering promorphinan/morphinan biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as part of the clustered promorphinan gene set rather than individually functionally revalidated in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct promorphinan intermediate toward thebaine and downstream morphinans
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Orthologues identified only in subset of Clade 2 promorphinan/morphinan-producing species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in pathway-positive species by transcriptome/genome analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to conversion of salutaridine toward thebaine-producing branch
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled long-day growth; metabolites measured in latex and capsules
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative metabolite profiling; transcriptomics; genome synteny and orthology analyses
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and genome data under BioProject PRJNA770669
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SALR encodes salutaridine reductase, converting salutaridine to salutaridinol. 2. Orthologues co-occur with STORR and other promorphinan genes in a subset of Clade 2 Papaver species producing promorphinan/morphinan compounds. 3. P. bracteatum shows good synteny with opium poppy regions and contains an extra copy of SALR.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary downstream enzyme to combine with STORR and other promorphinan genes for reconstructing morphinan flux
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Copy-number variation was observed in P. bracteatum.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Orthologues identified only in subset of Clade 2 promorphinan/morphinan-producing species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in pathway-positive species by transcriptome/genome analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports synthesis of the immediate precursor to thebaine in pathway-positive Papaver species
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled long-day growth; metabolites measured in latex and capsules
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative metabolite profiling; transcriptomics; genome synteny and orthology analyses
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and genome data under BioProject PRJNA770669
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SALAT encodes salutaridinol 7-O-acetyltransferase, producing salutaridinol-7-O-acetate from salutaridinol. 2. Orthologues are restricted to a subset of Clade 2 species with promorphinan/morphinan pathway capacity. 3. In P. setigerum chromosome 8, two annotated SALAT-like genes were reinterpreted as fragments of a single SALAT pseudogene.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important pathway-stacking component for metabolic engineering of thebaine/morphinan production; annotation quality should be verified due to pseudogenization risk
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Pse08G45690.0 and Pse08G45700.0 were reported as likely fragments of one intronless SALAT pseudogene containing frameshift and nonsense mutations.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the morphinan intermediate thebaine
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Orthologues identified only in subset of Clade 2 promorphinan/morphinan-producing species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in pathway-positive species by transcriptome/genome analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Thebaine is a morphinan alkaloid and pharmaceutical precursor related to analgesic opioid alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables thebaine formation in Papaver species possessing the full promorphinan gene complement
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled long-day growth; metabolites measured in latex and capsules
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative metabolite profiling; transcriptomics; genome synteny and orthology analyses
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and genome data under BioProject PRJNA770669
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THS encodes thebaine synthase and catalyzes conversion of salutaridinol-7-O-acetate to thebaine. 2. THS orthologues were found only in a subset of Clade 2 Papaver species with STORR and promorphinan/morphinan metabolites. 3. Clustering of THS with STORR and other promorphinan genes formed before divergence of P. bracteatum and P. somniferum.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Terminal pathway enzyme needed for engineering thebaine-producing lines together with upstream STORR/SALSYN/SALR/SALAT
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied mainly via comparative genomics and co-occurrence with metabolites rather than direct enzymology in this paper.
